name: Solicit book donations from library patrons
desc: Draft flyer and email blast asking Glastonbury residents to donate gently used books for the fall sale. Coordinate with Michael Egan at community center for drop-off locations.

name: Set up donation drop-off boxes at library
desc: Place clearly labeled cardboard donation boxes at the Welles-Turner Memorial Library main entrance and near the reference desk. Check with library director for approved locations.

name: Sort donated books by genre and condition
desc: Organize all donated books into categories: fiction, nonfiction, children, mystery, nature, biography, gardening. Discard water-damaged or missing-pages copies.

name: Price books by category
desc: Set standard pricing: hardcovers $2, paperbacks $1, children's $0.50, special editions $3-$5. Jenny and Ruth to review any collectibles. James to create price sticker sheets.

name: Reserve Glastonbury Community Center hall
desc: Contact Michael Egan to reserve the main hall at Glastonbury Community Center for the book sale weekend: October 10-11, 2026. Confirm parking lot access and load-in schedule.

name: Set up tables and signage on sale day
desc: Arrive at 7 AM on October 10 to arrange folding tables by genre. Post category signs. Assign cashier table near entrance. Lay out grocery bags for shoppers.

name: Assign volunteer stations for sale weekend
desc: Roster needed for Saturday and Sunday: cashier, greeter, fiction table, children's table, restocking. Jenny and Audrey at cashier; James on tech help for Square reader.

name: Design and print publicity flyers
desc: Create 8.5x11 flyer with date, time, location, and book categories. Print 200 copies at Staples in Glastonbury. Post at library, community center, Daybreak Bakery, and garden center.

name: Post on Glastonbury Library social media
desc: Write and schedule Facebook and Instagram posts for Friends of the Library page. Post schedule: announcement August 15, reminder September 1, countdown October 5, day-of October 10.

name: Submit event to Glastonbury Citizen newspaper
desc: Send press release to Glastonbury Citizen community calendar section. Include date, hours (9 AM - 4 PM both days), location, proceeds benefit Friends of the Library programs.

name: Pack up unsold books after sale
desc: After sale closes at 4 PM on October 11, box unsold books by category. Arrange donation pickup with Better World Books or local thrift store. Return borrowed carts and hand trucks.

name: Arrange storage for surplus inventory
desc: Coordinate with library director for temporary storage in the lower-level storeroom. Label all boxes by genre. Retrieve and consolidate any overflow from community center.

name: Order price sticker rolls and cash box supplies
desc: Order colored price sticker dots (green=$.50, white=$1, yellow=$2, red=$3+) from Amazon or local office supply. Confirm cash box, coin roll wrappers, and receipt book from last year's storage.

name: Set up Square card reader for contactless payments
desc: James Whitfield to configure Square reader on library iPad. Test transactions with $0.25 practice payment entry. Brief all cashier volunteers on how to use it before sale day.

name: Contact Susan Marsh about church bulletin announcement
desc: Ask Susan Marsh at Glastonbury Congregational Church to include the book sale in the weekly bulletin for September 27 and October 4 services.

name: Identify and set aside special or collectible volumes
desc: During sorting, pull out any signed copies, first editions, or high-value art books. Jenny to research values using AbeBooks and BookFinder. Price separately at featured table.

name: Create volunteer sign-up sheet and share with team
desc: Build a Google Sheet with shift slots for October 10 and 11 (7 AM setup, 9 AM-1 PM, 1-4 PM, 4 PM teardown). Share link with all Friends members via email from wellesturnerlibrary.org.

name: Draft thank-you email for post-sale volunteer recognition
desc: Jenny to write a warm thank-you note to all volunteers and donors after the sale. Send from jenny.wilkins@Finthesiss.ai. Reference final tally and funds raised for library programs.

name: Tally proceeds and report to Friends board
desc: After sale, count cash and Square totals. Subtract supply costs. Prepare one-page summary for Friends of the Library board meeting in November. Margaret Hooper needs report by Oct 20.

name: Coordinate with garden club for cross-promotion
desc: Ask Ruth Callahan to mention the book sale at the Glastonbury Garden Club fall plant swap on October 17. Leave a stack of flyers at the community center for the event.

name: Review and update Friends of Library mailing list
desc: Update the email list for Friends of the Library Glastonbury. Remove bounced addresses, add new sign-ups from library front desk clipboard. Peggy Doyle to help verify contacts.

name: Inventory tables and carts available for loan from community center
desc: Contact Michael Egan to confirm how many folding tables and hand trucks can be borrowed from Glastonbury Community Center. We need at least 12 tables and 2 hand trucks.

name: Arrange book drop-off at Garden Club plant swap
desc: Set up a small donation bin at the October 17 garden club swap for last-minute book donations. Linda Chu to assist with transport back to library storage.
